target_notes: Snapshot Serengeti is a citizen-science camera-trap dataset from Serengeti National Park, Tanzania. 11 seasons of data (S01-S11) collected 2010-2014; ~3.2M images annotated by Zooniverse volunteers. Documented in Swanson et al. (2015), 'Snapshot Serengeti, high-frequency annotated camera trap images of 40 mammalian species in an African savanna'. The published 60-class taxonomy includes 'empty' (camera triggered but no animal visible), 'human' (fieldworkers/poachers), and 58 wildlife species. Wildebeest tops the non-empty count list because of the Serengeti's famous ~1.5M-strong wildebeest migration. Captured the species list + train/val splits from Lila BC (lila.science) — both are public no-auth Azure Blob downloads.
capture_method: GET 2 small files from Lila BC: species_list.csv (894 bytes, 60 species + image counts) and SnapshotSerengetiSplits_v0.json (4.7 KB, train/val site splits). The full S01-S11 image archive is hundreds of GB; the per-season annotation JSONs are 19 MB+ each — both deliberately not captured in v0. case_002 may add one season's annotations once cassette discipline is wired.
